input_text:
Establishing the architecture rule for our new Python microservices: async/await throughout, no mixing of sync and async code within a service.

The reason: mixing sync and async code is a common source of bugs. Calling a sync blocking function from an async context blocks the entire event loop. It also makes the codebase harder to reason about — you have to track which functions are async-safe.

The rule: new Python services use asyncio throughout. All I/O (database, HTTP, Redis) uses async libraries (asyncpg, httpx, aioredis). If a library is sync-only, it goes behind a thread pool executor.

expected.text: New Python microservices use async/await throughout (asyncpg, httpx, aioredis). Sync-only libraries must be wrapped in a thread pool executor — no mixing sync and async in the same service

reviewer_notes: Architecture rule for new Python services.
